items.title: VI Live: Man zet PSV in de slotfase naast Volendam, ploeg van Bosz wil meer
items.summary: PSV gaat vrijdagavond op bezoek bij FC Volendam. De Eindhovenaren willen de grote voorsprong weer wat uitbreiden met het oog op een extreem vroege landstitel. Volg de wedstrijd vanaf 20.00 uur op de voet in de VI Live.

items.title: Hull City v Chelsea: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup fourth-round updates, 7.45pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽ Live scoreboard | Follow us on Bluesky | Email John Hull have been a considerable talent school in recent years: Harry Maguire, Jarrod Bowen, Andy Robertson and lately Keane Lewis-Potter have all carved decent careers in the Premier League. Tom Cairney, too. Liam Rosenior has FA Cup heritage, and played in this Wembley final classic in 2014. City were unlucky in this game, very unlucky. Continue reading...

items.title: Slot rueful as Liverpool injury bug strikes again
items.summary: Arne Slot has said the "last thing" Liverpool need this season is another injury after midfielder Wataru Endo was stretchered off against Sunderland.

items.title: Sources: Spurs finalizing Tudor as interim boss
items.summary: Tottenham are finalizing the appointment of Igor Tudor as interim head coach until the end of the season, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Van Persie: Sterling at Feyenoord to 'regain joy'
items.summary: Feyenoord boss Robin van Persie has said that Raheem Sterling chose to join the Eredivisie club to "regain the joy" in his football.

items.title: PSG stunned by coachless Rennes in title blow
items.summary: Coachless Rennes stunned Ligue 1 leaders Paris Saint-Germain 3-1 at home and snapped a five-game winless run on Friday.

items.title: Madrid-linked Upamecano signs new Bayern deal
items.summary: France defender Dayot Upamecano has signed a new four-year deal to stay at Bayern Munich, ending speculation that he could join Real Madrid or Paris Saint-Germain.

items.title: PSG zet koppositie op het spel na uitglijder in Bretagne
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain heeft vrijdagavond verrassend verloren op bezoek bij Stade Rennes. In het Roazhon Park won Rennes met 3-1 van de kampioen, die zijn koppositie in Ligue 1 zo op het spel zet. RC Lens kan later dit weekend de leiderstrui overnemen.

items.title: Gigantische blunder Jong PSV-keeper Ngom helpt De Graafschap in het zadel
items.summary: De Graafschap kende vrijdag een lastig begin op bezoek bij Jong PSV, maar de club uit Doetinchem kwam op heel schlemielige wijze langszij. Een enorme blunder betekende de 1-1.

items.title: 'Intense, aggressive, entertaining' - what will Tudor bring to Spurs ?
items.summary: After Igor Tudor agrees to take charge of Tottenham for the remainder of the season, BBC Sport takes a look at what supporters can expect to see from his side.

items.title: Vaessen gecounterd: 'Het is een privilege om voor Fortuna Sittard te spelen'
items.summary: In een kleedkamer die al jaren veel weg heeft van een duiventil, is Ivo Pinto de stabiele factor. De 36-jarige Portugees is bezig aan zijn vijfde seizoen bij Fortuna Sittard. Hij spreekt FC Groningen-doelman Etienne Vaessen tegen.

items.title: Man cave and fatherhood - Elanga on life at Newcastle
items.summary: Why Anthony Elanga is loving his time at Newcastle following his £55m move from Nottingham Forest in the summer.

items.title: Warrington Wolves with early lead vs St Helens LIVE!
Feyenoord onthult: Sterling neemt rugnummer vertrokken Carranza over
items.summary: Raheem Sterling gaat bij Feyenoord voetballen met rugnummer 19. Dat heeft de Rotterdamse club vrijdagmiddag wereldkundig gemaakt.

items.title: Will Hearts topple the Old Firm and end long wait for Scottish league title? – video
items.summary: Ewan Murray discusses the reasons for Hearts' success this season and why Derek McInnes's side should be considered favourites as they hope to become the first team outside of Celtic and Rangers to win the Scottish top flight since Aberdeen in 1985 Continue reading...

items.title: When to watch final F1 pre-season test on Sky Sports
items.summary: Formula 1's preparations for its new era in 2026 come to a conclusion with one last test in Bahrain as the new racing campaign draws ever nearer.

items.title: Pereira signs deal as Forest boss until end of next season
items.summary: Vitor Pereira has signed a deal until the end of next season to become Nottingham Forest's fourth manager of the campaign.

items.title: Hull in title contention as Rhodes drops down to 13th after leading in Riyadh
items.summary: Charley Hull put herself in title contention at the PIF Saudi Ladies International in Riyadh but there was disappointment for Mimi Rhodes who dropped from leader to 13th.

items.title: Mercedes top last day of Bahrain test - but Russell warns of 'scary' rival
items.summary: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli and George Russell set the fastest times on the final day of the first Bahrain test ahead of Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton amid continued claims and counter claims from F1's four leading teams about who is really 2026's early leading force.

items.title: Blaming immigrants for problems is wrong, says Pep Guardiola after Ratcliffe comments
items.summary: ‘Society is better when we embrace other cultures’ Haaland doubt for FA Cup tie, Rodri charged by FA Pep Guardiola has said that blaming people from overseas for a country’s problems is wrong, the Manchester City manager’s comments coming amid the fallout of Sir Jim Ratcliffe claiming the UK is being “colonised by immigrants”. Ratcliffe’s comment, made in an interview with Sky News on Wednesday, has led to widespread condemnation, including from within football, leading to Manchester United’s single largest minority owner saying he was sorry that his “choice of language has offended some people in the UK and Europe”. Continue reading...
